meta.ministry: Ministry of Personnel, Public Grievances and Pensions, Department of Administrative Reforms & Public Grievances
meta.project_subject: Rendering consultancy/project services of one person as PMU for 60 man-days in IE&C Division for the work relating to India's election to the post of President, IIAS
meta.deliverables: The PMU professional will assist the IE&C Division of DARPG in all aspects of the election campaign for the Presidency of IIAS. Key deliverables include preparing presentations, documentation, and related materials; engaging with IIAS members, affiliated bodies, nation states, individuals, organizations, and institutions worldwide to campaign for India's candidacy; conducting frequent meetings and active collaboration with IIAS and its sister organizations. The professional must have at least one year of experience at QCI, report directly to the Chairman and Secretary General of QCI, and possess core expertise in data analytics and documentation. The engagement spans 60 man-days with a mid-term review after 1.5 months.

content.full_text: The Department of Administrative Reforms & Public Grievances (DARPG) under the Ministry of Personnel, Public Grievances and Pensions issued this work order on 3 April 2025 to the Quality Council of India (QCI). The order engages QCI to provide one professional as a Project Management Unit (PMU) resource for 60 man-days to support India's election campaign for the President position at the International Institute of Administrative Sciences (IIAS). The Secretary of DARPG is the candidate. The PMU professional will assist with preparing presentations, documentation, and global stakeholder engagement. The resource must be a QCI professional with at least one year of tenure, reporting directly to QCI's Chairman and Secretary General, and must have data analytics and documentation expertise. The total cost is Rs 5,04,000 excluding GST, with QCI also receiving 12% administration cost. Payment is milestone-based: 25% each at work-order award, completion of one month, two months, and three months. A review may be conducted after 1.5 months, and disputes will be resolved mutually.
